channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: The Epic Games Mega Sale is now on with weekly free games
item.content:encoded:
Epic is getting the jump on the inevitable rush of sales for Summer Game Fest with the start of the Epic Games Mega Sale, bringing discounts of up to 75% off PC games, as well as the store’s usual clutch of free game giveaways and more.

The sale went live on 14th May and will run until 11th June at 11AM ET / 4PM BST / 5PM CEST.

Highlighted in the sale are:

  • Stellar Blade – £40.19 (33% off)
  • Cyberpunk 2077 – £17.49 (65% off) with Adam Smasher Outfit and Shoulder Cannon Backbling in Fortnite as a gift with purchase
  • Silent Hill 2 – £29.99 (50% off)
  • The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt Complete Edition – £6.99 (80% off)
  • Ghost of Tsushima Director’s Cut – £29.99 (40% off)
  • Super Meat Boy 3D – £15.99 (20% off) with a Super Meat Boy Sidekick in Fortnite as a gift with purchase
  • John Carpenter’s Toxic Commando – £27.99 (20% off)
  • Norse: Oath of Blood – £20.24 (25% off)

But you really need to just head to the mega sale’s page here.

The first duo of free games are Sunderfolk and Batman The Telltale Series Shadows Edition, both of which can be redeemed until 21st May. There’s also Epic Extras with bonus freebies for games, from a wrap for Fortnite and Triplex Black Wheels for Rocket League, to extras for Mongil Star Drive, Infinity Nikki, Neverwinter and more.

Source: Epic Games



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/16/the-epic-games-mega-sale-is-now-on-with-weekly-free-games/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Moss: The Forgotten Relic brings the mousey VR saga to flatscreen consoles and PC
item.content:encoded:
Some of the most beloved and acclaimed VR games are coming to flatscreen PC and consoles this summer, as Polyarc has announced Moss: The Forgotten Relic.

Moss: The Forgotten Relic is an all-in-one collection of Moss, Moss: Book II and the Twilight Garden DLC, adapted and enhanced to be payable on PC and consoles without needing a VR headset.

It will be launching across PC, PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch 1 and 2 this summer.

This is great news for getting more people to experience this charming adventure, especially as the market shifts away from virtual reality gaming, consigning it to be a niche interest. Moss was a game that already took a light touch with its virtual reality dynamics, a puzzle platformer presenting you with interactive diorama-like scenes to guide your mouse hero through and to reach into with sight and motion controls. Some elements will need to be reworked and adapted for flatscreen and pure controller play,

Looking back on the original’s 2018 release for PSVR, Jim said, “Moss is an absolute joy to play, proving that a great VR experience doesn’t always require that you flail your arms around while fending off bouts of motion sickness. It’s a wonderfully realised puzzle platformer with clever twists, beautiful backdrops, and an adorable hero, making PlayStation VR even more tempting for prospective buyers.”

via Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/17/moss-the-forgotten-relic-brings-the-mousey-vr-saga-to-flatscreen-consoles-and-pc/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
